1	import { Schema, Struct } from "effect";
2	import { ProjectId, ThreadId, TrimmedNonEmptyString } from "./baseSchemas";
3	
4	import {
5	  ClientOrchestrationCommand,
6	  OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput,
7	  ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS,
8	  OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput,
9	  OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput,
10	  OrchestrationReplayEventsInput,
11	} from "./orchestration";
12	import {
13	  GitCheckoutInput,
14	  GitCreateBranchInput,
15	  GitCreateWorktreeInput,
16	  GitInitInput,
17	  GitListBranchesInput,
18	  GitPullInput,
19	  GitRemoveWorktreeInput,
20	  GitRunStackedActionInput,
21	  GitStatusInput,
22	} from "./git";
23	import {
24	  TerminalClearInput,
25	  TerminalCloseInput,
26	  TerminalOpenInput,
27	  TerminalResizeInput,
28	  TerminalRestartInput,
29	  TerminalWriteInput,
30	} from "./terminal";
31	import { KeybindingRule } from "./keybindings";
32	import { ProjectSearchEntriesInput, ProjectWriteFileInput } from "./project";
33	import { OpenInEditorInput } from "./editor";
34	
35	// ── WebSocket RPC Method Names ───────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	export const WS_METHODS = {
38	  // Project registry methods
39	  projectsList: "projects.list",
40	  projectsAdd: "projects.add",
41	  projectsRemove: "projects.remove",
42	  projectsSearchEntries: "projects.searchEntries",
43	  projectsWriteFile: "projects.writeFile",
44	
45	  // Shell methods
46	  shellOpenInEditor: "shell.openInEditor",
47	
48	  // Git methods
49	  gitPull: "git.pull",
50	  gitStatus: "git.status",
51	  gitRunStackedAction: "git.runStackedAction",
52	  gitListBranches: "git.listBranches",
53	  gitCreateWorktree: "git.createWorktree",
54	  gitRemoveWorktree: "git.removeWorktree",
55	  gitCreateBranch: "git.createBranch",
56	  gitCheckout: "git.checkout",
57	  gitInit: "git.init",
58	
59	  // Terminal methods
60	  terminalOpen: "terminal.open",
61	  terminalWrite: "terminal.write",
62	  terminalResize: "terminal.resize",
63	  terminalClear: "terminal.clear",
64	  terminalRestart: "terminal.restart",
65	  terminalClose: "terminal.close",
66	
67	  // Server meta
68	  serverGetConfig: "server.getConfig",
69	  serverUpsertKeybinding: "server.upsertKeybinding",
70	} as const;
71	
72	// ── Push Event Channels ──────────────────────────────────────────────
73	
74	export const WS_CHANNELS = {
75	  terminalEvent: "terminal.event",
76	  serverWelcome: "server.welcome",
77	  serverConfigUpdated: "server.configUpdated",
78	} as const;
79	
80	// -- Tagged Union of all request body schemas ─────────────────────────
81	
82	const tagRequestBody = <const Tag extends string, const Fields extends Schema.Struct.Fields>(
83	  tag: Tag,
84	  schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>,
85	) =>
86	  schema.mapFields(
87	    Struct.assign({ _tag: Schema.tag(tag) }),
88	    // PreserveChecks is safe here. No existing schema should have checks depending on the tag
89	    { unsafePreserveChecks: true },
90	  );
91	
92	const WebSocketRequestBody = Schema.Union([
93	  // Orchestration methods
94	  tagRequestBody(
95	    ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.dispatchCommand,
96	    Schema.Struct({ command: ClientOrchestrationCommand }),
97	  ),
98	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getSnapshot, OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput),
99	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getTurnDiff, OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput),
100	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getFullThreadDiff, OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput),
101	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.replayEvents, OrchestrationReplayEventsInput),
102	
103	  // Project Search
104	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsSearchEntries, ProjectSearchEntriesInput),
105	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsWriteFile, ProjectWriteFileInput),
106	
107	  // Shell methods
108	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.shellOpenInEditor, OpenInEditorInput),
109	
110	  // Git methods
111	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitPull, GitPullInput),
112	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitStatus, GitStatusInput),
113	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRunStackedAction, GitRunStackedActionInput),
114	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitListBranches, GitListBranchesInput),
115	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateWorktree, GitCreateWorktreeInput),
116	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRemoveWorktree, GitRemoveWorktreeInput),
117	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateBranch, GitCreateBranchInput),
118	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCheckout, GitCheckoutInput),
119	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitInit, GitInitInput),
120	
121	  // Terminal methods
122	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalOpen, TerminalOpenInput),
123	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalWrite, TerminalWriteInput),
124	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalResize, TerminalResizeInput),
125	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClear, TerminalClearInput),
126	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalRestart, TerminalRestartInput),
127	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClose, TerminalCloseInput),
128	
129	  // Server meta
130	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverGetConfig, Schema.Struct({})),
131	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverUpsertKeybinding, KeybindingRule),
132	]);
133	
134	export const WebSocketRequest = Schema.Struct({
135	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
136	  body: WebSocketRequestBody,
137	});
138	export type WebSocketRequest = typeof WebSocketRequest.Type;
139	
140	export const WebSocketResponse = Schema.Struct({
141	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
142	  result: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
143	  error: Schema.optional(
144	    Schema.Struct({
145	      message: Schema.String,
146	    }),
147	  ),
148	});
149	export type WebSocketResponse = typeof WebSocketResponse.Type;
150	
151	export const WsPush = Schema.Struct({
152	  type: Schema.Literal("push"),
153	  channel: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
154	  data: Schema.Unknown,
155	});
156	export type WsPush = typeof WsPush.Type;
157	
158	// ── Union of all server → client messages ─────────────────────────────
159	
160	export const WsResponse = Schema.Union([WebSocketResponse, WsPush]);
161	export type WsResponse = typeof WsResponse.Type;
162	
163	// ── Server welcome payload ───────────────────────────────────────────
164	
165	export const WsWelcomePayload = Schema.Struct({
166	  cwd: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
167	  projectName: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
168	  bootstrapProjectId: Schema.optional(ProjectId),
169	  bootstrapThreadId: Schema.optional(ThreadId),
170	});
171	export type WsWelcomePayload = typeof WsWelcomePayload.Type;

{

**Key Pattern**: Uses subpath exports to control what consumers can import. Direct TypeScript source files are exposed (no build step).

#### **packages/contracts/package.json**
```json
{
  "name": "@t3tools/contracts",
  "version": "0.0.8",
  "private": true,
  "files": ["dist"],
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./src/index.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./src/index.ts",
      "import": "./src/index.ts",
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "tsdown src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts --watch --clean",

  }
}
```

**Key Pattern**: Default export only (no subpaths). Uses `tsdown` to build ESM and CJS bundles. Types always point to source.

### 4. PACKAGES/SHARED - DETAILED EXPORTS AND CONTENT

#### **src/model.ts** - Model utility functions
Exports:
- `getModelOptions(provider: ProviderKind)` - Get available models for provider
- `getDefaultModel(provider: ProviderKind)` - Get default model for provider
- `normalizeModelSlug(model, provider)` - Normalize model name to canonical slug
- `resolveModelSlug(model, provider)` - Resolve to valid model or default
- `resolveModelSlugForProvider(provider, model)` - Wrapper for provider-specific resolution
- `getReasoningEffortOptions(provider)` - Get reasoning effort levels
- `getDefaultReasoningEffort(provider)` - Get default reasoning effort
- Re-exports: `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS`

**Depends on**: `@t3tools/contracts` (model types and constants)

#### **src/git.ts** - Git branch utilities
Exports:
- `sanitizeBranchFragment(raw: string)` - Clean branch name fragment (lowercase, max 64 chars)
- `sanitizeFeatureBranchName(raw: string)` - Format as `feature/…` branch
- `resolveAutoFeatureBranchName(existingBranchNames, preferredBranch)` - Find unique feature branch with collision detection

**Pure utility functions** - no external dependencies except types.

#### **src/logging.ts** - Rotating file sink
Exports:
- `RotatingFileSink` - Class for rotating log files
  - Constructor options: `filePath`, `maxBytes`, `maxFiles`, `throwOnError?`
  - `write(chunk)` - Write string/Buffer to log
  - Auto-rotates when size exceeds maxBytes
  - Prunes old backup files

**Depends on**: Node.js `fs` and `path` modules

#### **src/shell.ts** - Shell PATH extraction
Exports:
- `extractPathFromShellOutput(output: string)` - Extract PATH value between markers
- `readPathFromLoginShell(shell: string, execFile?)` - Execute shell with `-ilc` to read login PATH

**Depends on**: Node.js `child_process` module

#### **src/Net.ts** - Network/port utilities (Effect-based)
Exports:
- `NetError` - Tagged error class
- `NetService` - Effect service with methods:
  - `canListenOnHost(port, host)` - Check if port is bindable
  - `isPortAvailableOnLoopback(port)` - Check both IPv4 and IPv6 localhost
  - `reserveLoopbackPort(host?)` - Allocate and release ephemeral port
  - `findAvailablePort(preferred)` - Find available port (prefers specified, falls back to 0)

**Depends on**: Node.js `net` module, Effect library

#### **src/index.ts** - Barrel exports (default export)

#### **src/baseSchemas.ts** - Branded type definitions
Exports (all based on Effect Schema):

- `TrimmedNonEmptyString` - Non-empty trimmed string
- `NonNegativeInt` - Int >= 0
- `PositiveInt` - Int >= 1
- `IsoDateTime` - ISO date string

#### **apps/web** (React frontend)

- Imports from contracts: types for orchestration, terminal, git, provider schemas
- Imports from shared: model utilities for resolving model slugs

Example files consuming these:
- `nativeApi.ts` - Uses `NativeApi` type from contracts
- Various state management and components use schema types

#### **apps/server** (Node.js backend)
Dependencies (devDependencies):
- `@t3tools/contracts`: "workspace:*"

Usage examples from grep:
- `main.ts`: Imports `NetService` from `@t3tools/shared/Net`

#### **apps/desktop** (Electron app)
Dependencies (devDependencies):
- `@t3tools/contracts`: "workspace:*"
- `@t3tools/shared`: "workspace:*"

---

### 7. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

#### **Subpath Exports (packages/shared)**
```typescript
// Consumer can only import from specific subpaths
import { NetService } from "@t3tools/shared/Net";
import { RotatingFileSink } from "@t3tools/shared/logging";
import { normalizeModelSlug } from "@t3tools/shared/model";
// NOT: import { NetService } from "@t3tools/shared"; // Would fail
```

#### **Single Default Export (packages/contracts)**
```typescript
// All types available via barrel import
import { ThreadId, ProviderKind, NativeApi } from "@t3tools/contracts";
```

#### **Effect Schema-based Contracts**
All domain types use Effect Schema for:
- Type definition and validation
- Runtime schema validation
- Codec support (encoding/decoding)
- Branded types for IDs

#### **Workspace Dependencies**
Both packages use `"workspace:*"` to reference each other and catalog dependencies:
```json
"dependencies": {
  "@t3tools/contracts": "workspace:*",
  "effect": "catalog:"
}
```

#### **Direct TypeScript Source**
- packages/shared exposes `.ts` files directly (no build)
- packages/contracts uses `tsdown` to build CommonJS + ES Modules
- Both expose `.ts` source for types in exports

---

### 8. NOTABLE CONSTRAINTS AND VALIDATIONS

From baseSchemas and throughout contracts:
- All IDs must be trimmed, non-empty strings
- Keybindings max 256 items
- Terminal cols: 20-400, rows: 5-200
- Chat input: max 120,000 characters
- Images: max 10MB
- When expressions: max depth 64
- Branch names: max 64 characters after sanitization
- Project search: max 200 results
- Model slugs normalized via alias mapping

---

### 9. BUILD & TEST CONFIGURATION

**packages/shared**:
- Scripts: prepare (effect-language-service), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test (vitest run)
- Uses vitest + @effect/vitest for Effect testing

**packages/contracts**:
- Scripts: dev (tsdown with watch), build (tsdown esm+cjs), prepare, typecheck, test
- Uses tsdown for dual ESM/CJS build
- Effect language service plugin enabled with strict rules

---

This comprehensive structure enables:
1. **Type-safe contracts** between frontend, backend, and desktop
2. **Schema validation** at runtime using Effect Schema
3. **Shared utilities** for common operations (model resolution, git operations, networking)
4. **Modular imports** via subpath exports in shared
5. **Complete domain modeling** for orchestration, providers, terminals, and projects

  - `ProviderKind` - "codex" literal
  - `ProviderApprovalPolicy` - "untrusted", "on-failure", "on-request", "never"
  - `ProviderSandboxMode` - "read-only", "workspace-write", "danger-full-access"
  - `ProviderServiceTier` - "fast", "flex"
  - `RuntimeMode` - "approval-required", "full-access"
  - `ProviderInteractionMode` - "default", "plan"
  - `ProviderRequestKind` - "command", "file-read", "file-change"
  - `ProviderApprovalDecision` - "accept", "acceptForSession", "decline", "cancel"

- **Chat Domain**:
  - `ChatAttachment` - currently union of ChatImageAttachment only
  - `ChatImageAttachment` - type, id, name, mimeType, sizeBytes
  - `UploadChatImageAttachment` - includes dataUrl

- **Project Domain**:
  - `ProjectScript` - id, name, command, icon, runOnWorktreeCreate
  - `ProjectScriptIcon` - "play", "test", "lint", "configure", "build", "debug"
  - `OrchestrationProject` - project metadata with scripts array

- **Message & Conversation**:
  - `OrchestrationMessageRole` - "user", "assistant", "system"
  - `OrchestrationMessage` - id, role, text, attachments, turnId, streaming
  - `OrchestrationProposedPlan` - id, turnId, planMarkdown

- **Session & Thread State**:
  - `OrchestrationSessionStatus` - "idle", "starting", "running", "ready", "interrupted", "stopped", "error"
  - `OrchestrationSession` - threadId, status, providerName, runtimeMode, activeTurnId

#### **src/server.ts** - Server configuration schemas
Exports:
- **Issues**: `ServerConfigIssue` - Union of keybindings issues
- **Provider Status**:
  - `ServerProviderStatusState` - "ready", "warning", "error"
  - `ServerProviderAuthStatus` - "authenticated", "unauthenticated", "unknown"
  - `ServerProviderStatus` - includes provider, status, auth status, availability
  
- **Configuration**:
  - `ServerConfig` - cwd, keybindingsConfigPath, keybindings, issues array, providers
  - `ServerUpsertKeybindingInput` - KeybindingRule
  - `ServerUpsertKeybindingResult` - keybindings and issues
  - `ServerConfigUpdatedPayload` - issues and providers for updates

#### **src/keybindings.ts** - Keybinding configuration schemas
Constants:
- `MAX_KEYBINDING_VALUE_LENGTH` - 64
- `MAX_WHEN_EXPRESSION_DEPTH` - 64
- `MAX_SCRIPT_ID_LENGTH` - 24
- `MAX_KEYBINDINGS_COUNT` - 256
- `STATIC_KEYBINDING_COMMANDS` - terminal.toggle, terminal.split, chat.new, etc.

Exports:
- `KeybindingCommand` - Union of static commands + script.{id}.run pattern
- `KeybindingRule` - key, command, when (optional)
- `KeybindingsConfig` - Array of rules (max 256)
- `KeybindingShortcut` - parsed key binding with modifier flags
- `KeybindingWhenNode` - recursive AST for when expressions (identifier, not, and, or)
- `ResolvedKeybindingRule` - command, shortcut, whenAst
- `ResolvedKeybindingsConfig` - array of resolved rules

#### **src/project.ts** - Project search and file operations
Constants:
- `PROJECT_SEARCH_ENTRIES_MAX_LIMIT` - 200
- `PROJECT_WRITE_FILE_PATH_MAX_LENGTH` - 512

Exports:
- `ProjectSearchEntriesInput` - cwd, query (max 256), limit
- `ProjectEntry` - path, kind (file|directory), parentPath
- `ProjectSearchEntriesResult` - entries array, truncated flag
- `ProjectWriteFileInput` - cwd, relativePath, contents
- `ProjectWriteFileResult` - relativePath

#### **src/ws.ts** - WebSocket protocol schemas
Exports:
- **Methods**: `WS_METHODS` object with RPC method names for:
  - projects (list, add, remove, searchEntries, writeFile)
  - shell (openInEditor)
  - git (pull, status, runStackedAction, listBranches, createWorktree, etc.)
  - terminal (open, write, resize, clear, restart, close)
  - server (getConfig, upsertKeybinding)

- **Channels**: `WS_CHANNELS` - terminalEvent, serverWelcome, serverConfigUpdated

- **Request/Response**:
  - `WebSocketRequest` - id, body (tagged union of all request types)
  - `WebSocketResponse` - id, result?, error?
  - `WsPush` - type: "push", channel, data
  - `WsResponse` - Union of response and push

- **Welcome**: `WsWelcomePayload` - cwd, projectName, bootstrapProjectId?, bootstrapThreadId?

#### **src/ipc.ts** - Desktop IPC bridge (complex interfaces)
Exports:
- `NativeApi` - Complete interface for desktop/electron communication:
  - dialogs (pickFolder, confirm)
  - terminal (open, write, resize, clear, restart, close, onEvent)
  - projects (searchEntries, writeFile)
  - shell (openInEditor, openExternal)
  - git (all git operations)
  - contextMenu (show)
  - server (getConfig, upsertKeybinding)
  - orchestration (snapshot, dispatch, getTurnDiff, getFullThreadDiff, replayEvents, onDomainEvent)

- `DesktopBridge`, `DesktopRuntimeInfo`, `DesktopUpdateState`, `DesktopUpdateActionResult` - Electron-specific types
- `ContextMenuItem` - generic context menu item

---

### 6. HOW THESE PACKAGES ARE CONSUMED

#### **apps/web** (React frontend)
Dependencies:
- `@t3tools/contracts`: "workspace:*"
- `@t3tools/shared`: "workspace:*"

Usage patterns from grep:
- Imports from contracts: types for orchestration, terminal, git, provider schemas
- Imports from shared: model utilities for resolving model slugs

Example files consuming these:
- `nativeApi.ts` - Uses `NativeApi` type from contracts
- Various state management and components use schema types

#### **apps/server** (Node.js backend)
Dependencies (devDependencies):
- `@t3tools/contracts`: "workspace:*"
- `@t3tools/shared`: "workspace:*"

Usage examples from grep:
- `main.ts`: Imports `NetService` from `@t3tools/shared/Net`
- `open.ts`: Imports `EDITORS`, `EditorId` from `@t3tools/contracts`
- `codexAppServerManager.ts`: Imports `normalizeModelSlug` from `@t3tools/shared/model`
- `provider/EventNdjsonLogger.ts`: Imports `RotatingFileSink` from `@t3tools/shared/logging`
- Multiple files use schema types (ThreadId, ProviderKind, etc.) from contracts

#### **apps/desktop** (Electron app)
Dependencies (devDependencies):
- `@t3tools/contracts`: "workspace:*"
- `@t3tools/shared`: "workspace:*"

---

### 7. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

#### **Subpath Exports (packages/shared)**
```typescript
// Consumer can only import from specific subpaths
import { NetService } from "@t3tools/shared/Net";
import { RotatingFileSink } from "@t3tools/shared/logging";
import { normalizeModelSlug } from "@t3tools/shared/model";
// NOT: import { NetService } from "@t3tools/shared"; // Would fail
```

#### **Single Default Export (packages/contracts)**
```typescript
// All types available via barrel import
import { ThreadId, ProviderKind, NativeApi } from "@t3tools/contracts";
```

#### **Effect Schema-based Contracts**
All domain types use Effect Schema for:
- Type definition and validation
- Runtime schema validation
- Codec support (encoding/decoding)
- Branded types for IDs

#### **Workspace Dependencies**
Both packages use `"workspace:*"` to reference each other and catalog dependencies:
```json
"dependencies": {
  "@t3tools/contracts": "workspace:*",
  "effect": "catalog:"
}
```

#### **Direct TypeScript Source**
- packages/shared exposes `.ts` files directly (no build)
- packages/contracts uses `tsdown` to build CommonJS + ES Modules
- Both expose `.ts` source for types in exports

---

### 8. NOTABLE CONSTRAINTS AND VALIDATIONS

From baseSchemas and throughout contracts:
- All IDs must be trimmed, non-empty strings
- Keybindings max 256 items
- Terminal cols: 20-400, rows: 5-200
- Chat input: max 120,000 characters
- Images: max 10MB
- When expressions: max depth 64
- Branch names: max 64 characters after sanitization
- Project search: max 200 results
- Model slugs normalized via alias mapping

---

### 9. BUILD & TEST CONFIGURATION

**packages/shared**:
- Scripts: prepare (effect-language-service), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test (vitest run)
- Uses vitest + @effect/vitest for Effect testing

**packages/contracts**:
- Scripts: dev (tsdown with watch), build (tsdown esm+cjs), prepare, typecheck, test
- Uses tsdown for dual ESM/CJS build
- Effect language service plugin enabled with strict rules

---

This comprehensive structure enables:
1. **Type-safe contracts** between frontend, backend, and desktop
2. **Schema validation** at runtime using Effect Schema
3. **Shared utilities** for common operations (model resolution, git operations, networking)
4. **Modular imports** via subpath exports in shared
5. **Complete domain modeling** for orchestration, providers, terminals, and projects

## Summary of findings

### Existing theme system

- Design tokens cover background, foreground, primary, secondary, muted, accent, border, input, ring, and more
- The settings page at `_chat.settings.tsx` already has an Appearance section with System/Light/Dark radio options using a `THEME_OPTIONS` array
- The `packages/shared` directory uses subpath exports while `packages/contracts` uses a single barrel export with schema-based types

I need to figure out where to define the theme type. The instruction says to put it in the shared package, which uses subpath exports rather than a barrel export, so I'll create a new `theme.ts` file there and add it to the exports. The tricky part is that `useTheme.ts` currently defines theme as just "light" | "dark" | "system", but the new system needs to support something more flexible.

I'm shifting from a simple toggle to named custom themes with specific color palettes. So I'll define `ThemeId` as a string literal union, create a `ThemeColors` interface with all the color slots, and a `ThemeDef` that combines the id, label, and colors. Then I'll export a `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array with the three required themes: "t3-dark", "t3-light", and "t3-midnight". For the hook, I'll either update `useTheme.ts` or create a new `useCustomTheme.ts` that handles persisting the selected theme to localStorage and applying the CSS custom properties to the DOM.

The existing system uses a `.dark` class on the `<html>` element with CSS variables defined in `index.css`, but the new approach should apply CSS custom properties directly for each named theme. I need to decide whether to replace the current light/dark/system system entirely or extend it, and I'm leaning toward replacing it since the new named themes are more flexible and can cover all the color slots needed for the sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar.

The task specifies three custom themes—"t3-dark", "t3-light", and "t3-midnight"—which are distinct from the generic OS-preference modes. I think the cleanest solution is to embed the existing light/dark/system concept within this new theme system, where "t3-dark" and "t3-light" map to their respective palettes, "t3-midnight" provides a very deep dark option, and the new hook can handle system preference detection internally to pick the appropriate theme automatically.

Defining the theme architecture... I'm realizing the existing CSS already relies on the `.dark` class for dark mode detection, and Tailwind's dark variants depend on that class being present. So I need to decide whether to keep that approach for backward compatibility while adding theme-specific CSS custom properties on top, or migrate everything to CSS custom properties and remove the `.dark` class dependency entirely. The current setup uses `@custom-variant dark` to target the `.dark` class, and `@theme inline` already leverages CSS custom properties for Tailwind colors, so there's flexibility in how I approach this.

Looking at the existing tokens—background, foreground, primary, secondary, muted, accent, card, popover, border, input, and ring—I need to extend these with sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar colors. The cleanest solution is to keep the existing CSS custom properties as the foundation for all Tailwind classes, define complete sets of custom properties for each named theme, and when a theme is selected, apply the appropriate `.dark` or `.light` class while overriding the custom properties to match that theme's palette. For the default themes like "t3-dark" and "t3-light", they'd just use the existing dark and light CSS from the index file, while "t3-midnight" would get its own distinct set of custom properties.

But the task description makes it clear that each theme should define its own complete set of colors across all categories. So I'm restructuring this: I'll create a theme module in `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` that exports a `ThemeColors` interface covering all the color tokens, a `ThemeDef` interface for theme definitions, and a `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array with the three themes fully specified. This keeps the architecture clean without requiring build config changes, and each theme can apply its colors through CSS custom properties.

For the hook, I'm reconsidering whether to use Context since the task explicitly asks for a theme provider and useTheme hook. The existing `useSyncExternalStore` pattern works, but adding Context would be more conventional and align better with the stated requirements. existing `useTheme` works without a Context provider (it uses `useSyncExternalStore`). Here's the complete plan for the custom theme system:

---

## What I'll build (8 files: 4 new, 4 modified)

### New files

1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Plain TypeScript `ThemeId` union, `ThemeColorTokens` interface, `BuiltInTheme` interface, and the `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array with concrete colour values for all three themes. Includes `isThemeId()` guard and `getBuiltInTheme()` utility.

2. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useCustomTheme.ts`** — Module-singleton hook (mirrors the existing `useTheme.ts` pattern). Calls `applyCustomTheme()` at import-time for FOUC prevention, toggles both `data-theme` attribute and `.dark` class, cross-tab sync via `storage` event, and `useSyncExternalStore` for React reactivity.

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React Context wrapper over `useCustomTheme()`. Exposes `{ themeId, setThemeId, theme, themes }` and a `useThemeContext()` hook.

4. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Three-column grid of swatch cards. Each card shows a mini preview (sidebar strip + editor panel + accent dot), the theme label, and an "Active" badge.

### Modified files

5. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Add `"./theme"` subpath export.

6. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Add `:root[data-theme="t3-light"]`, `[data-theme="t3-dark"]`, and `[data-theme="t3-midnight"]` rule sets after the existing `:root` block. Each overrides all existing CSS tokens plus adds 5 new semantic tokens (`--theme-sidebar-bg`, `--theme-editor-bg`, `--theme-terminal-bg`, `--theme-status-bar-bg`, `--theme-status-bar-fg`). Uses `:root[data-theme]` specificity (0,2,0) so cascade order beats the existing `@variant dark` block — no `!important` needed.

7. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrap `RootRouteView` return with `<ThemeProvider>` at the outermost level.

8. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Remove old `THEME_OPTIONS` radio group and `useTheme` import; replace the Appearance section body with `<ThemeSelector />`.

## Custom Theme System — Implementation Complete

All 8 files from the plan have been created/updated:

### New files

1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Defines `ThemeId` (`"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"`), `ThemeColorTokens` interface (background, foreground, accent, sidebarBg, editorBg, terminalBg, statusBarBg, statusBarFg, dark), `BuiltInTheme`, the `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array with concrete colour values, plus `isThemeId()` guard and `getBuiltInTheme()` utility.

2. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useCustomTheme.ts`** — Module-singleton hook following the existing `useTheme.ts` pattern. Calls `applyCustomTheme()` at import-time for FOUC prevention, sets `data-theme` attribute + toggles `.dark` class, writes 5 semantic CSS custom properties to `:root` inline style, cross-tab sync via `storage` event, and `useSyncExternalStore` for React reactivity.

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — React Context wrapper over `useCustomTheme()`. Exposes `{ themeId, setThemeId, theme, themes }` via `useThemeContext()` hook.

Uses inline styles for the preview so swatches always show the real theme colours regardless of the active theme.

6. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Added three `:root[data-theme="…"]` rule sets (specificity 0,2,0) that override all CSS tokens plus the 5 new `--theme-*` semantic tokens for each built-in theme.

7. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrapped `RootRouteView` return with `<ThemeProvider>` at the outermost level.

8. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Removed `THEME_OPTIONS`, `useTheme` import, and the old radio group. Replaced the Appearance section body with `<ThemeSelector />`.
